ir_specs.statement: label LABEL Each address may be tagged with a label string.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: label LABEL Each address may be tagged with a label string.

ir_specs.statement: The maximum allowed total length of label is 15 characters.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: The maximum allowed total length of label is 15 characters.

ir_specs.statement: scope SCOPE_VALUE the scope of the area where this address is valid.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: scope SCOPE_VALUE the scope of the area where this address is valid.

ir_specs.statement: The available scopes are listed in /usr/lib/iproute2/rt_scopes or /etc/iproute2/rt_scopes (has precedence if exists).
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: The available scopes are listed in /usr/lib/iproute2/rt_scopes or /etc/iproute2/rt_scopes (has precedence if exists).

ir_specs.statement: Predefined scope values are: global - the address is globally valid.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Predefined scope values are: global - the address is globally valid.

ir_specs.statement: site - (IPv6 only, deprecated) the address is site local, i.e.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: site - (IPv6 only, deprecated) the address is site local, i.e.

ir_specs.statement: it is valid inside this site.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: it is valid inside this site.

ir_specs.statement: link - the address is link local, i.e.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: link - the address is link local, i.e.

ir_specs.statement: it is valid only on this device.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: it is valid only on this device.

ir_specs.statement: host - the address is valid only inside this host.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: host - the address is valid only inside this host.

ir_specs.statement: metric NUMBER priority of prefix route associated with address.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: metric NUMBER priority of prefix route associated with address.

ir_specs.statement: valid_lft LFT the valid lifetime of this address; see section 5.5.4 of RFC 4862.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: valid_lft LFT the valid lifetime of this address; see section 5.5.4 of RFC 4862.

ir_specs.statement: When it expires, the address is removed by the kernel.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: When it expires, the address is removed by the kernel.

ir_specs.statement: preferred_lft LFT the preferred lifetime of this address; see section 5.5.4 of RFC 4862.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: preferred_lft LFT the preferred lifetime of this address; see section 5.5.4 of RFC 4862.

ir_specs.statement: When it expires, the address is no longer used for new outgoing connections.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: When it expires, the address is no longer used for new outgoing connections.
